# UC-22.4.17 · CCPA/CPRA — Minor Consent — Age-Gating API Denials vs Account Creation Success Mismatch

## Description

Selling or sharing minors’ PI requires affirmative authorization under specified conditions. Detecting account creation paths that succeed when age-gate APIs return under-16/under-13 outcomes indicates broken client flows or server-side bypass.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for CCPA/CPRA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes app and sourcetypes auth:signup, identity:age_check. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Align `session_id` correlation; (2) immediate P1 to product security; (3) block accounts via batch job downstream; (4) legal determines applicable age threshold by business line; (5) never log raw DOB in Splunk—use `age_band` only. Indexes required: `index=app`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=auth:signup`, `sourcetype=identity:age_check`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Application logs via HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=app` `sourcetype="auth:signup"` (session_id, account_created, _time); `index=app` `sourcetype="identity:age_check"` (session_id, age_band, decision, _time).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Align `session_id` correlation; (2) immediate P1 to product security; (3) block accounts via batch job downstream; (4) legal determines applicable age threshold by business line; (5) never log raw DOB in Splunk—use `age_band` only.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=app sourcetype="identity:age_check" decision="under16" earliest=-30d
| join type=left session_id [
    search index=app sourcetype="auth:signup" account_created=true earliest=-30d
    | fields session_id, account_created, _time
    | rename _time as signup_time
  ]
| where isnotnull(account_created)
| table session_id, decision, signup_time, account_created
```

Understanding this SPL

**CCPA/CPRA — Minor Consent — Age-Gating API Denials vs Account Creation Success Mismatch** — Selling or sharing minors’ PI requires affirmative authorization under specified conditions. Detecting account creation paths that succeed when age-gate APIs return under-16/under-13 outcomes indicates broken client flows or server-side bypass.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=app` `sourcetype="auth:signup"` (session_id, account_created, _time); `index=app` `sourcetype="identity:age_check"` (session_id, age_band, decision, _time). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Application logs via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: app; **sourcetype**: identity:age_check. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=app, sourcetype="identity:age_check", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(account_created)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: account_created, decision, session_id, signup_time.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (incidents), Single value (count), Timeline.

## SPL

```spl
index=app sourcetype="identity:age_check" decision="under16" earliest=-30d
| join type=left session_id [
    search index=app sourcetype="auth:signup" account_created=true earliest=-30d
    | fields session_id, account_created, _time
    | rename _time as signup_time
  ]
| where isnotnull(account_created)
| table session_id, decision, signup_time, account_created
```

## Visualization

Table (incidents), Single value (count), Timeline. Key fields: `account_created`, `decision`, `session_id`, `signup_time`.
